zh:
「怪谈」一词在江户时代定型：夏夜纳凉，众人围坐点起百根灯芯，每讲完一个鬼故事吹熄一根，讲到第一百根时怪异降临——「百物语」的游戏规则本身就是这个体系的宣言：恐怖是用来轮流讲述、共同消费的。四谷怪谈的阿岩、皿屋敷的阿菊、牡丹灯笼的阿露并称江户三大怪谈，在歌舞伎的机关布景、净琉璃的三味线与圆朝的落语里反复上演；含冤而死的女人化作怨灵复仇，是这批故事共享的骨架。1690年刊的《死灵解脱物语闻书》与1727年的《四谷杂谈集》表明，江户人讲鬼故事时惯于声称「实有其事」，纪实的口吻从一开始就是怪谈文体的一部分。

近代以后这条线并未断绝，只是换了载体。明治年间，井上圆了创「妖怪学」逐条解剖狐狗狸，小泉八云用英文重述雪女与无耳芳一；战后，怪谈搬进新建的学校校舍——厕所里的花子、红斗篷、玛丽小姐的电话在1970至80年代的孩子间口耳相传，1990年常光彻《学校的怪谈》出版后汇成出版与影视的洪流。1979年的裂口女恐慌则展示了都市传说的另一种威力：一则流言让全国小学组织护送放学，也给社会心理学留下了教科书级的传播样本。

2000年前后，2ch的灵异板成为新的围炉：如月车站的实时失踪直播、八尺样的乡村结界、Kunekune的禁忌之视，都诞生于匿名论坛的接力叙事，读者既是听众也是共作者。从百物语的灯芯到深夜的电话铃、再到手机屏幕上的时间戳，怪谈始终在用最新的日常器物改装最旧的恐惧；它也是本图鉴中唯一至今仍在高速增殖的体系——下一个条目，也许此刻正在某个论坛上被敲出来。
en:
The word kaidan took its modern shape in the Edo period. On summer nights people gathered to play hyakumonogatari: a hundred lit wicks, one ghost story told and one wick blown out at a time, with something uncanny promised at the hundredth. The game's rules are the tradition's manifesto — horror as something told in turns and consumed together. Oiwa of Yotsuya Kaidan, Okiku of the Dish Mansion, and Otsuyu of the Peony Lantern, the three great ghost tales of Edo, were staged and restaged with kabuki's trick scenery, the puppet theater's shamisen, and Encho's rakugo; a wronged woman returning as a vengeful spirit is the shared skeleton. Documentary framing was there from the start: the Shiryo Gedatsu Monogatari Kikigaki of 1690 and the Yotsuya Zatsudanshu of 1727 both insist that their hauntings really happened.

The line never broke in the modern era; it changed carriers. In Meiji, Inoue Enryo founded 'monsterology' to dissect the Kokkuri-san seance, while Lafcadio Hearn retold the Snow Woman and Hoichi the Earless in English. After the war the ghosts moved into the new school buildings: Hanako of the toilet, Aka Manto, and Mary-san's telephone passed from child to child through the 1970s and 80s, converging into a publishing and film flood after Tsunemitsu Toru's Gakko no Kaidan appeared in 1990. The kuchisake-onna panic of 1979 showed the other face of the genre, a single rumor that had schools nationwide organizing escorted walks home and left social psychology a textbook case of transmission.

Around 2000 the occult boards of 2channel became the new hearth. Kisaragi Station's live-posted disappearance, Hasshaku-sama's warded village, Kunekune's forbidden sight — all grew out of anonymous relay narration, where every reader is also a co-author. From the wicks of hyakumonogatari to the ring of a night telephone to timestamps on a forum thread, kaidan keeps refitting the oldest fears into the newest household objects, and it is the one system in this atlas still multiplying at speed: the next entry may be getting typed on some board right now.
